worldview.subtitleSuffix: — comparisons for this verse under this worldview are listed here.
worldview.descGuiltInnocence: Guilt–Innocence cultures understand life primarily through the lens of moral law, personal responsibility, and ethical right and wrong. When someone violates a standard, the result is guilt — an internal sense of having broken what is just. Restoration comes through confession, forgiveness, and being declared innocent again. This worldview emphasises truth, justice, fairness, and the individual's standing before what is right.
worldview.descShameHonour: Shame–Honour cultures see identity as deeply connected to community, family, and social harmony. The central question is not 'Am I right?' but 'Do I belong, and do I bring honour to my people?' Wrongdoing brings shame — a loss of face, dignity, or relational standing. Restoration comes through acceptance, reconciliation, and the recovery of honour. This worldview emphasises loyalty, respect, reputation, and the healing of relationships.
worldview.descFearPower: Fear–Power cultures understand the world as a place shaped by spiritual forces, both good and harmful. The central concern is not guilt or shame but vulnerability — the fear of unseen powers that influence daily life. People seek protection, blessing, and strength through a greater power that can overcome what threatens them. Restoration comes through deliverance, spiritual authority, and the assurance of divine presence. This worldview emphasises protection, victory, and the reality of the spiritual realm.
worldview.loading: Hang tight — we're gathering voices from across traditions and weaving this worldview's perspective together. Rich insights are on their way.

howItWorks.intro: VerseSmart brings together trusted Christian commentators from around the world and shows you how their insights connect, differ, and interact — all in one place.
howItWorks.forEachVerse: Every time you open a verse, VerseSmart:
howItWorks.bullet1: Brings together diverse commentators from different cultures, traditions, and backgrounds
howItWorks.bullet2: Identifies the key themes they're discussing
howItWorks.bullet4: Reveals the voices behind each theme, showing why perspectives vary
howItWorks.bullet5: Maps each commentator by Region, Tradition, Denomination, and Worldview
howItWorks.bullet6: Highlights global patterns in how Christians interpret the same passage
howItWorks.tabsHeading: You can explore the commentary through five clear tabs:
howItWorks.tabWorldviewDesc: compare Guilt–Innocence, Shame–{honour}, and Fear–Power perspectives
howItWorks.tabTraditionDesc: trace insights across classic, contemporary, and historical streams
howItWorks.tabDenominationDesc: understand how different church families approach the text
howItWorks.coloursHelp: Colours help you read at a glance:
howItWorks.closing: VerseSmart gives you a structured, global, multi-perspective view of Scripture — helping you see the Bible through the eyes of the worldwide Church.

whyChoose.body1: VerseSmart gives you a neutral space to explore Scripture without the influence of personalised AI history.
whyChoose.body2: Every comparison you see is shaped only by the verse itself — not by your past searches, preferences, or behaviour. Your lookups remain completely anonymous, so your study stays private and untracked.
whyChoose.body3: It's also a place to organise your study neatly. You can return to your favourite verses, revisit insights, and explore consistent worldview perspectives across Scripture — all in one clean, uncluttered space designed to help you focus on what matters.

pricingPage.title: Simple, fair pricing
pricingPage.subtitle1: All plans include the complete library of theologians and global perspectives.
pricingPage.subtitle2: Choose the plan that matches how deeply you want to study — from occasional lookups to unlimited exploration with saved history.
pricingPage.engageFeature1: Plenty of daily comparisons for consistent study
pricingPage.engageFeature3: Perfect for daily readers, small‑group prep, and deeper exploration
pricingPage.exploreFeature1: Study freely without worrying about limits
pricingPage.exploreFeature2: Full access to all voices and perspectives
pricingPage.exploreFeature3: 100 verse look-up saved in your history (across all translations)
pricingPage.exploreFeature4: Ideal for deep study, teaching prep, and regular exploration
pricingPage.exploreFeature5: Hand-pick commentators to compare in every verse lookup.
pricingPage.exploreFeature6: Full control of commentary comparisons across traditions, regions, and denominations.
pricingPage.cancelLine: Cancel anytime. Payments handled by Paddle (our Merchant of Record). 30-day refund window —
